articles.title: What are the main events for today?
articles.snippet: EUROPEAN SESSIONIn the European session, we don't have much on the agenda other than the French business confidence data which is not going to change anything for the ECB, so the market reaction will be muted. AMERICAN SESSIONIn the American session, we get the weekly US ADP jobs data and the US Consumer Confidence report. articles.title: European Parliament postpones vote on EU-US trade deal - report
articles.snippet: Everything about how Europe has negotiated tariffs has been a disaster. They were the one jurisdiction with the market size and diversification to stand up to US tariffs and they folded in pathetic fashion despite the likelihood -- now proven -- that the US Supreme Court would strike down tariffs.Now they want a do-over and, luckily their terrible deal with the US hasn't been ratified by European Parliament so they will get it. However now they've irked the US and missed their best opportunity t

articles.title: EUR/USD: Supreme Court tariff ruling and Fed speaker lineup drive midweek volatility
articles.snippet: The Euro (EUR) is trading near the 1.1775 level on Tuesday after recovering from a one-month low last week.

articles.title: European gas: Weather relief but new risks – Commerzbank
articles.snippet: Commerzbank’s Norman Liebke notes European gas prices have stabilized as milder weather reduces withdrawals from storage, with low withdrawal rates likely to persist in coming weeks.

articles.title: Galapagos Reports Full Year 2025 Financial Results and Provides Fourth Quarter Business Update
articles.snippet: Mechelen, Belgium; February 23, 2026, 22:01 CET; regulated information – Galapagos NV (Euronext & NASDAQ: GLPG) today reported its financial results for the full year 2025 and provided an update on the fourth quarter 2025 and its year-to-date performance.

[0.67] European Parliament postpones vote on EU-US trade deal - report
Everything about how Europe has negotiated tariffs has been a disaster. They were the one jurisdiction with the market size and diversification to stand up to US tariffs and they folded in pathetic fashion despite the likelihood -- now proven -- that the US Supreme Court would strike down tariffs.Now they want a do-over and, luckily their terrible deal with the US hasn't been ratified by European Parliament so they will get it. [0.67] EUR/USD: Supreme Court tariff ruling and Fed speaker lineup drive midweek volatility
The Euro (EUR) is trading near the 1.1775 level on Tuesday after recovering from a one-month low last week.

[0.67] European gas: Weather relief but new risks – Commerzbank
Commerzbank’s Norman Liebke notes European gas prices have stabilized as milder weather reduces withdrawals from storage, with low withdrawal rates likely to persist in coming weeks.

[0.67] Galapagos Reports Full Year 2025 Financial Results and Provides Fourth Quarter Business Update
Mechelen, Belgium; February 23, 2026, 22:01 CET; regulated information – Galapagos NV (Euronext & NASDAQ: GLPG) today reported its financial results for the full year 2025 and provided an update on the fourth quarter 2025 and its year-to-date performance.
